title: CandleRuntime end-to-end integration smoke test (Mamba2 + RWKV + Transformer + hooks)
goal: End-to-end smoke test for CandleRuntime covering all three model families: Transformer load+generate+hook+LoRA; Mamba2 load+generate+state_vector commit/restore; RWKV v5/v6/v7 load+generate. Test gated by per-family env vars (graceful skip per family).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/tests/candle_e2e_smoke.rs with per-family sub-tests gated by env vars; tests/README.json updated with per-family env var list.
implementation_notes: Smoke scenarios per family: (Transformer) load Llama-style via HANDSHAKE_TEST_CANDLE_MODEL_DIR; capabilities check; generate; capture activations at layer N; register zero-vector steering at N + generate (identity); register random vector + generate (output differs); mount LoRA + generate (output differs); unmount; unload. (Mamba2) load via HANDSHAKE_TEST_MAMBA2_MODEL_DIR; capabilities (subquad=true); generate 32 tokens deterministically; state_vector_commit; generate; state_vector_restore; generate same continuation; tamper hash test; unload. (RWKV v5) load via HANDSHAKE_TEST_RWKV_V5_MODEL_DIR; generate; state commit/restore; unload. (RWKV v6) same. (RWKV v7) same. Each sub-test skips gracefully if its env var unset; CI without any GPU env vars still runs the test binary (all sub-tests skip with status SKIPPED logged). FR event family coverage asserted per family. README.json updated: { test_id: "candle_e2e_smoke", required_env: ["HANDSHAKE_TEST_CANDLE_MODEL_DIR", "HANDSHAKE_TEST_MAMBA2_MODEL_DIR", "HANDSHAKE_TEST_RWKV_V5_MODEL_DIR", "HANDSHAKE_TEST_RWKV_V6_MODEL_DIR", "HANDSHAKE_TEST_RWKV_V7_MODEL_DIR"], description: "per-family skip if unset" }.
risk_if_missed: CandleRuntime regressions ship undetected; AC-CANDLE-ADAPTER has no integration evidence across families.
validator_focus: Per-family skip path documented; FR event family coverage asserted per family; identity test (zero-vector hook -> output unchanged) is the correctness gate.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Per-family skip path documented and tested (no panic on missing env var).
FR event family coverage asserted per family.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Per-family env-gated skip pattern is correct (each sub-test returns SKIPPED if its env var is unset; CI without any env vars still compiles and runs the test binary as all-SKIPPED); per-family event_family coverage strings are asserted; README.json contract is verified by a dedicated README test; family enumeration is the same in test code and README. CONCERN: (a) The state-vector sub-tests for Mamba2/RWKV are structurally insufficient (see MT-088 FAIL) — they pass without actually proving state restore works because generate always resets state; this MT inherits the defect. (b) The transformer sub-test asserts `assert_eq!(baseline, zero, "zero-vector steering must preserve deterministic transformer output")` which is the identity-test correctness gate the contract calls out — but the gate runs only when HANDSHAKE_TEST_CANDLE_MODEL_DIR is set; default CI runs all-SKIPPED, so the gate is never enforced in the default build. (c) The transformer sub-test mounts a LoRA at width-by-1 rank-1 dims (vec![0.5; width] reshaped to (width, 1)) — fine as a smoke target but does not assert the LoRA-applied output DIFFERS from baseline; the `_lora` result is discarded.
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-INT-002 — smoke tests do not assert ContextBundle -> ModelAdapter -> ToolGate -> ArtifactStore wire. They only exercise direct ModelRuntime calls; the actual obligation wire test is absent.
